description_long: This is a small, white house with a steep gable roof, heavily overgrown with green vines and scattered purple flowers. The house features a simple front porch with a white railing and a single blue door, accompanied by a small rectangular window. The vines climb up all sides of the house, partially obscuring the windows and roof, and cascade down the walls, giving the structure an abandoned or reclaimed-by-nature appearance. The roof is composed of evenly spaced, horizontal panels, and the house sits on a flat, green base that resembles a manicured lawn. The overall design is minimalistic, with clean lines and a focus on the contrast between the stark white structure and the organic, wild vegetation. The house appears to be a single-story dwelling, suitable for a suburban or semi-rural neighborhood.
description: A small, white gable-roofed house overgrown with green vines and purple flowers, featuring a blue door and a simple porch. The structure is minimalistic and appears reclaimed by nature.
description_view_0: Front view showing the blue door, porch with white railing, a window, and dense vine growth on the facade and roof.
description_view_1: Side view dominated by thick vines cascading down the wall and roof, with the porch partially visible on the left.
description_view_2: Rear view with no visible doors, a single window, and extensive vine coverage obscuring much of the wall.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the gable end, heavy vine growth, and a glimpse of the porch and blue door on the right.
attribute_entryway_design: Simple blue door with a small porch, white railing, and no overhang, partially obscured by vines.
